title: Dashboard
footnote: All figures come from the invoice ledger — the same numbers as the Invoices view. Debtors is the current outstanding total, independent of the period.

tiles.wipSub: {{count}} open cards — accepted or in progress, not invoiced
tiles.warrantyCost: true cost: {{parts}} parts + {{hours}} labour ≈ {{value}} of capacity
tiles.warrantyHonesty: Rework invoices at £0 by design — this is what it cost to do the work again: parts at cost, labour at your rate.

capacity.title: Capacity
capacity.note: Cumulative labour hours across the month — where your capacity is versus what you've taken on and billed.
capacity.explainer: Capacity pace is your sellable hours accruing day by day, reaching the month's total on the last working day (flat on weekends, bank holidays and closed days). Committed is labour hours taken on — job cards accepted or in progress, dated by their diary date; it is hours taken on, not hours worked (there is no time clocking). Billed is hours actually charged, dated by invoice date. Capacity pace above Committed means the diary isn't full. Committed above Billed means work is done but not yet invoiced. Billed lagging Committed in a sawtooth (work Monday, invoice Friday) is normal — a gap that never closes is the signal.

pnl.title: Profit & loss
pnl.monthlyNote: Monthly figures — whole calendar months only (labour costs are a monthly wage bill).
pnl.uncostedParts: {{count}} parts line(s), {{retail}} retail, have no recorded cost — shown at 100% margin
pnl.uncostedPartsHelp: These parts bring in revenue but their trade cost isn't recorded, so the gross margin above treats them as pure profit — it is overstated by their real cost. Add each part to the catalogue to record its cost and correct the margin.
pnl.hoursChargedSub: own mechanics’ billable hours — fixed-service hours + ad-hoc labour (outsourced and warranty rework excluded)
pnl.netProfitShort: short of covering the month so far — fixed costs shown in full
pnl.netProfitAhead: ahead — the month's fixed costs are covered
pnl.inProgressStrip: {{elapsed}} of {{total}} days · fixed costs shown in full
pnl.stillToSellValue: {{money}} at your labour rate, if sold
pnl.stillToSellHelp: Sellable capacity left for the rest of this month, valued at your labour rate. 'Already booked' is diary time reserved (bay occupancy) — a different measure, shown alongside, never subtracted.
pnl.honesty: Labour and net figures come from your Headcount and Overheads settings. Charged hours come from the labour hours stored on each job line when the service was added — editing a service in Products affects future jobs only, never past ones.
pnl.degradedNote: P&L is whole calendar months only — showing {{label}}.
pnl.utilSetupLink: tick chargeable mechanics and set contracted hours in HR →
pnl.utilCalc: {{mechanics}} mechanic(s) over {{days}} rostered days − {{leave}} leave − {{ph}} bank holidays = {{raw}} raw; × utilisation factors = {{available}} sellable; {{charged}} charged ÷ {{available}} = {{pct}}
pnl.costBaseSub: wages + overheads — paid every month whether you sell anything or not. Parts aren’t included: they’re variable, and already deducted in gross margin.
pnl.breakEvenSub: hours to sell this month just to stand still
pnl.breakEvenNoRate: no labour rate set for {{sites}} — that share isn’t counted
pnl.breakEvenRevenue: As revenue (÷ this month’s margin rate): {{value}}
pnl.breakEvenResidual: Refined (parts & outsourced margin already covers some of it): {{hours}}h of labour
pnl.breakEvenHonesty: Charged hours only count what’s invoiced through GreaseDesk — provisional until all billing moves here; the arithmetic isn’t.
pnl.hoursWentSub: absence this month — leave, sickness and bank holidays. Reduces capacity; not lost sales.
pnl.wfFraming: Leave and bank holidays reduce clock time; the utilisation factor then discounts what remains to realistically sellable hours. Neither is lost sales — the lost part is the unsold line.
pnl.unsoldSub: ≈ {{money}} of realistically sellable capacity that went unsold, at your labour rate
pnl.unsoldSubNoMoney: realistically sellable mechanic time that produced no invoice
pnl.unsoldCalc: {{available}} sellable − {{charged}} sold − {{rework}} rework = {{unsold}} unsold · × labour rate ≈ {{money}}
pnl.unsoldHonesty: Sellable hours are factor-adjusted — an 8-hour day at a 50% factor counts as 4 sellable hours, so this is capacity someone could realistically have billed, not raw clock time. GreaseDesk can’t yet say WHY it went unsold: work invoiced outside GreaseDesk, warranty rework (£0 by design, time still counted), unconverted diagnostics, or genuine idle time. Read it as a question to investigate, not a verdict — time tracking (planned) is what would answer it.
pnl.utilFactorHeading: Sellable = each person’s raw hours × their utilisation factor:
pnl.wfRework: warranty rework (hours spent redoing work for free)
pnl.utilReworkNote: {{hours}} warranty rework excluded from sold (spent capacity, not sold output)

notTrading: {{site}} isn’t trading this month — no invoices and no chargeable mechanics. Figures below show any costs apportioned to it.
